# Pull Request

**Summary**
What this PR changes, in 1–2 sentences.

**Motivation**
Why this change belongs in GitSpec. Link to the issue it resolves.

Closes #

**Type of change**
- [ ] `feat` — new functionality
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `chore` — tooling, CI, refactor with no behavior change
- [ ] `test` — tests only

**Layer(s) affected**
See the nine-layer architecture in `SKILL.md`:

**Checklist**
- [ ] I read `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
- [ ] I ran `bash ci/smoke.sh` locally and it passed.
- [ ] I ran `shellcheck` on any shell scripts I touched.
- [ ] I updated `CHANGELOG.md` under `[Unreleased]`.
- [ ] I updated `SKILL.md` or `README.md` if user-facing behavior changed.
- [ ] This PR does not add a runtime dependency, telemetry, or network call during install. (If it does, I opened a discussion first.)
